name: Pork Marsala
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch frying pan over medium heat, sauté mushrooms in butter for about 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently. Remove from the pan and set aside.
2. Slice pork into 1/4 inch thick medallions.
3. In the same pan over medium-high heat, sauté garlic in olive oil for about 2 minutes.
4. Add pork medallions, a few at a time, cooking each side for about 2 minutes. If needed, add more oil to prevent sticking.
5. Repeat step 4 until all pork is browned. Add the mushrooms and return them to the pan.
6. Reduce heat to low. Stir in chicken stock to deglaze the pan. Return the pork to the pan; add wine, salt, pepper, and mushrooms. Cover and cook for 20-25 minutes or until the pork is tender.
7. Serve with rice. Makes 4 servings.
